ToolSpecification.name: mosaic_list_risks
ToolSpecification.description:
The AWS Risk Library is an extensible reference library that contains potential risk events
that may impact AWS and/or its customers and the risk scenarios that could trigger them. The
library contains high-level risk categories (Level 1), (e.g., availability, security, third
party, etc.); sub-categories of risk events (Level 2) for each level 1 risk (e.g., network
failure, service failure, infrastructure failure); and plausible risk causes (Level 3) that
can result in a risk event (e.g., inadequate capacity planning, lack of governance oversight,
power outages, etc.). The level 2 risk events are the central element of the risk library.

This tool returns the risks that are part of the AWS Risk Library.

ToolSpecification.name: edit_quip_link_sharing
ToolSpecification.description:
Edit link sharing settings for an existing Quip document

This tool allows you to enable, disable, or change the link sharing mode
for an existing Quip document without modifying its content.

Parameters:
- documentId: The Quip document URL or ID
- mode: Link sharing mode ('view', 'edit', or 'none')

Examples:
1. Enable view-only link sharing:
```json
{
  "documentId": "https://quip-amazon.com/abc/Doc",
  "mode": "view"
}
```

2. Enable edit link sharing:
```json
{
  "documentId": "https://quip-amazon.com/abc/Doc",
  "mode": "edit"
}
```

3. Disable link sharing:
```json
{
  "documentId": "https://quip-amazon.com/abc/Doc",
  "mode": "none"
}
```

documentId.description: The Quip document URL or ID

mode.description: Link sharing mode: 'view' for view-only, 'edit' for edit access, 'none' to disable sharing

ToolSpecification.name: search_quip_commented_by_current_user
ToolSpecification.description:
Get all documents where the current user has left comments

This tool retrieves all Quip documents where the current user has posted comments.
You can optionally filter the results by date range to get documents with comments within a specific time period.

The tool checks all user-accessible threads for comments made by the current user,
with optional date range filtering for more targeted results.

Date format: Use ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) for date parameters.

Examples:
1. Get all documents with user comments:
```json
{
}
```

2. Get documents with comments within a date range:
```json
{
  "startDate": "2024-01-01",
  "endDate": "2024-12-31"
}
```

startDate.description: Start date for filtering comments (YYYY-MM-DD format)

endDate.description: End date for filtering comments (YYYY-MM-DD format)

ToolSpecification.name: read_quip_from_urls
ToolSpecification.description:
Extract and retrieve the full HTML content of Quip documents using their URLs

This tool reads multiple Quip documents simultaneously using their URLs.
It extracts document IDs from the provided links and retrieves the content
for all documents in a single operation.

The tool accepts an array of Quip document URLs and returns structured
information including document ID, title, content, and the original link
for each document.

Examples:
1. Read multiple documents:
```json
{
  "links": [
    "https://quip-amazon.com/abc/Document1",
    "https://quip-amazon.com/def/Document2"
  ]
}
```

links.description: Array of Quip document urls to read

ToolSpecification.name: create_quip
ToolSpecification.description:
Create a new Quip document or spreadsheet

This tool creates a new document or spreadsheet in Quip with the specified content.

Required parameters:
- content: The HTML or Markdown content of the new document (max 1MB)

Optional parameters:
- format: Format of the content ('html' or 'markdown', default is 'html')
- title: Title of the new document (max 10KB)
- member_ids: Comma-separated list of folder IDs or user IDs for access
- type: Type of document to create ('document' or 'spreadsheet', default is 'document')
- mode: Link sharing mode ('view', 'edit', or 'none' to disable sharing)

Notes:
- If title is not specified, it will be inferred from the first content
- If member_ids is not specified, the document will be created in the user's Private folder
- For spreadsheets, content must be surrounded by HTML <table> tags
- If mode is not specified, document uses default sharing settings

Examples:
1. Create a simple document:
```json
{
  "content": "# My New Document\n\nThis is a test document.",
  "format": "markdown"
}
```

2. Create a document with a title in a specific folder:
```json
{
  "content": "# Introduction\n\nThis is the start of my document.",
  "format": "markdown",
  "title": "Project Proposal",
  "member_ids": "ABCDEF123456"
}
```

3. Create a document with internal link sharing:
```json
{
  "content": "# Shared Document\n\nThis document has link sharing enabled.",
  "format": "markdown",
  "mode": "view"
}
```

4. Create a document with sharing disabled:
```json
{
  "content": "# Private Document\n\nThis document has no link sharing.",
  "format": "markdown",
  "mode": "none"
}
```

mode.description: Link sharing mode: 'view' for view-only, 'edit' for edit access, 'none' to disable sharing

member_ids.description: Comma-separated list of folder IDs or user IDs for access

type.description: Type of document to create

content.description: The HTML or Markdown content of the new document

ToolSpecification.name: mosaic_list_controls
ToolSpecification.description:
The AWS Control Library is the authoritative source of controls that AWS 
uses to manage operational risk. The library represents AWS's own control 
framework supporting high-level policies and standards, and represents 
management's directives and requirements that prescribe how the organization 
manages its risk and control processes. The library also provides a 
mapping of AWS controls to AWS' policies/standards, and external 
requirements such as regulatory and compliance frameworks. AWS implements 
these controls through various mechanisms, including architectural system 
design (e.g., region isolation), system enforced guardrails (e.g., static 
code analysis), or and centrally enforced organizational processes (e.g., 
application security reviews). Control owners, who are leaders at Level 8 
or above within the business, validate each control. The Security Assurance 
& Compliance (SA&C) team independently challenges these validations. To 
demonstrate assurance, each control includes a narrative that articulates 
how the control is implemented and supporting evidence of control execution 
that provides tangible proof of its implementation.

This tool returns the controls that are part of the AWS Control Library.

Results are organized to help quickly identify relevant scripts for specific tasks, with automatic handling of duplicate scripts across different directories. Ideal for exploring the script library or finding scripts based on filename patterns. Returns script names, paths, and descriptions to help users discover relevant scripts for their tasks. Categorize the scripts based on description.

ToolSpecification.name: isengard
ToolSpecification.description:
Access Amazon's internal Isengard service for AWS account management.
This tool is designed for builders including developers, support teams, and field teams (SAs and TAMs) 
to easily access their Isengard-managed AWS accounts, typically non-production accounts used for building and testing.

## When to use:
- When you need to list AWS accounts you own or have access to through POSIX group membership
- When you need detailed information about a specific Isengard-managed AWS account
- When you need temporary AWS credentials for testing or development work

## Limitations:
- Only works with Isengard-managed AWS accounts
- Requires appropriate permissions to the target AWS accounts, managed by Midway
- Credential access requires valid IAM role names already created for the AWS Account.
- This tool does not yet support alternative partitions such as GovCloud or China.
- listOwnedAWSAccounts supports pagination with maxResultsPerPage (1-100, default: 30) and maxPages (default: 1) parameters.

## Supported operations:
- listOwnedAWSAccounts: List all ACTIVE accounts you own with optional primary owner filtering and pagination
- getAWSAccount: Get detailed information about a specific AWS account
- getAssumeRoleCredentials: Get temporary AWS credentials for a specific account and IAM role

## Examples
List owned AWS accounts: isengard listOwnedAWSAccounts
Get AWS account details: isengard getAWSAccount --accountId 123456789012
Get AWS credentials for IAM role: isengard getAssumeRoleCredentials --accountId 123456789012 --roleName MyRole

maxResultsPerPage.description: Number of results per page (1-100, default: 30)

maxPages.description: Maximum number of pages to retrieve (default: 1)

roleName.description: IAM Role Name for getAssumeRoleCredentials operation.

ToolSpecification.name: add_comment_quip
ToolSpecification.description:
Add a comment to a Quip document

This tool allows you to add a comment to a specified Quip document or thread.
Comments appear in the thread's conversation panel and are visible to all document collaborators.
The comment will be attributed to the owner of the API token.

Parameters:
- threadIdOrUrl: (Required) The Quip document/thread ID or URL to add a comment to
- content: (Required) The comment message text to add
- section_id: ID of a document section to comment on

Notes:
- Plain text only, no formatting or HTML is supported
- Comments cannot be edited or deleted through the Quip API: These operations are not supported
- Maximum length is 1MB (though practical messages are typically much shorter)
- Only one of section_id or annotation_id can be provided
- annotation_id is retrieved as a response of the get_recent_messages_quip tool
- Manually creating a link to a quip section gives a response like : https://quip-amazon.com/bpVtAZ8LB0b4/Quip-Commenting-Capabilities-Test#fND9CAsTr5B
- Where bpVtAZ8LB0b4 is the threadId, and fND9CAsTr5B is the section_id.
- As such, the annotation_id is retreived by the get_recent_messages_quip tool

Examples:
1. Add a simple comment:
```json
{
  "threadIdOrUrl": "https://quip-amazon.com/abc/Doc",
  "content": "Great document! I have a few suggestions."
}
```

2. Add a comment to a specific section:
```json
{
  "threadIdOrUrl": "https://quip-amazon.com/abc/Doc",
  "content": "This section needs more detail.",
  "section_id": "SAf3351f25e51434479864cf71ce"
}
```

3. Reply to an existing comment:
```json
{
  "threadIdOrUrl": "https://quip-amazon.com/abc/Doc",
  "content": "I agree with your comment.",
  "annotation_id": "fND9CAeEYiG"
}
```

annotation_id.description: ID of a document comment to reply to

threadIdOrUrl.description: The thread ID or Quip URL to add a comment to

section_id.description: ID of a document section to comment on

content.description: The comment message content to add to the thread

ToolSpecification.name: read_permissions
ToolSpecification.description:
Read team information from Amazon's internal permissions system.

This tool allows you to retrieve detailed information about team memberships,
overrides, and rules from permissions.amazon.com team pages.

You MUST specify which tables OR rule sections to include in the response.
At least one of these parameters must be provided with at least one option selected.
The tool will only retrieve the specified tables and rule sections.

Available tables:
- additional_overrides: Additional Members overrides table
- deny_overrides: Denied Members overrides table
- team_membership: Team Membership table (large table, slow to retrieve)
- team_audit: Team Audit log table (very large table, very slow to retrieve)

Available rule sections:
- rule_membership: Membership rules section
- rule_additional_overrides: Additional Members overrides rules section

For large tables (especially team_membership and team_audit), you can use the
maxPages parameter to limit the number of pages processed and prevent timeouts.
You can also use tableFilters to narrow down the results.

maxPages.description: Maximum number of pages to process per table. Use for very large tables to prevent timeouts.

ruleSections.description: List of specific rule sections to include. At least one table or rule section must be specified.

tables.description: List of specific tables to include. At least one table or rule section must be specified.

tableFilters.description: Filters to apply to specific tables. Each filter contains a query string or array of query strings and optional threshold.

anyOf.description: Text to search for in the table rows
Multiple terms to search for in the table rows (combined with OR logic)

query.description: Text or array of texts to search for in the table rows

threshold.description: Fuzzy match threshold (0-1). Lower = stricter match. Default is 0.3

teamUrl.description: URL of the permissions team page to read

ToolSpecification.name: read_quip

Read Quip document content

This tool retrieves the content of a Quip document in either HTML or Markdown format:

- HTML format: More verbose but contains section IDs and additional metadata.
  These unique section IDs (for h1, h2, h3, p, etc.) can be used with the edit_quip tool
  to make targeted edits to specific sections of the document.

- Markdown format: More concise and easier to read, but does not contain section IDs
  or additional metadata. Best for when you just need the content in a readable format
  and don't need to make targeted edits.

Workflow:
1. Use read_quip to get the document content
2. Identify the section ID you want to modify (when using HTML format)
3. Use edit_quip with the section ID and appropriate location parameter

Examples:
1. Read document in HTML format (default):
```json
{
  "documentId": "https://quip-amazon.com/abc/Doc"
}
```

2. Read document in Markdown format:
```json
{
  "documentId": "https://quip-amazon.com/abc/Doc",
  "format": "markdown"
}
```

ToolSpecification.name: search_fleet_credit_score
ToolSpecification.description:
Retrieve operational credit scores for fleets managed by a specific Amazon manager, identified by their alias.
    This tool should be used when you want to evaluate the operational performance and creditworthiness of all fleets under a given manager. 
    The credit score here refers specifically to Amazon's internal fleet operational scoring system, **not** to a financial or consumer credit score.
    
    Each fleet's ID and associated operational credit score will be returned. 
    These scores help in identifying at-risk fleets and evaluating performance for compliance, reliability, and delivery operations.

    ### Use Cases:
    • "What are the credit scores of fleets managed by alias 'samishra@'?"
    • "Give me all fleet IDs and their scores under the manager 'samishra@'."
    
    ### When NOT to Use:
    • DO NOT use this tool to get personal or financial credit scores.
    • DO NOT use this tool if you don't have the manager alias.
    • NOT suitable for querying single fleet score (use a more targeted tool if available).
    
    ### Caveats:
    • Only works for Amazon-internal operational fleet credit score system.
    • The data may have a short refresh delay (up to 24 hours).
    • You must have permission to view data under the provided manager alias.

alias.description: Manager alias to fetch credit scores for

ToolSpecification.name: post_talos_correspondence
ToolSpecification.description:
Post correspondence on a Talos security task

This tool allows posting comments/correspondence on a specific Talos security task.
It uses the Talos API to create new correspondence entries for tasks.

Required parameters:
- taskId: ARN of the Talos task (format: arn:aws:talos-task:task/UUID)
- engagementId: ARN of the associated Talos engagement (format: arn:aws:talos-engagement:engagement/UUID)
- commentText: The comment text to post (max 10000 characters)

Example:
```json
{
  "taskId": "arn:aws:talos-task:task/5054ae8a-7eda-457f-991c-5ed40933f3ae",
  "engagementId": "arn:aws:talos-engagement:engagement/2498ed08-001c-4d89-a31b-6299c7822a0b",
  "commentText": "BSC17 compliance check completed. Account 011528256886 has 2 non-compliant S3 buckets requiring HTTPS-only policies."
}
```

Response:
On success, returns a JSON object with the correspondence ID and a preview of the posted comment.
On failure, returns an error message with details about what went wrong.

Limitations and Requirements:
- Requires valid Midway authentication (run `mwinit` if you encounter authentication errors)
- Limited to 10 requests per minute per user (rate limit)
- Comments cannot be edited or deleted through this tool once posted
- User must have appropriate permissions to access the specified Talos task and engagement
- Task and engagement must exist and be in a valid state to accept comments

When NOT to use this tool:
- Do not use for posting sensitive or classified information that should not be stored in Talos
- Do not use for posting large attachments or binary data (use the Talos UI directly instead)
- Do not use for bulk commenting on multiple tasks (use the Talos UI or API directly for batch operations)
- Do not use for retrieving task information (use the talos_get_task tool instead)

engagementId.description: ARN of the associated Talos engagement

taskId.description: ARN of the Talos task to post comment to

{ "client": "MyOrcaClient"}

{ "client": "MyOrcaClient", "workflow": "TestWorkflow", "status": "Normal" }

Example with context filtering:

```

```

ToolSpecification.name: read_kingpin_goal

ToolSpecification.name: sage_post_answer
ToolSpecification.description:
Post an answer to an existing question on Sage (Amazon's internal Q&A platform).

This tool allows you to contribute answers to questions on Sage through the MCP interface.
The answer content supports Markdown formatting for rich text, code blocks, and links.

Authentication:
- Requires valid Midway authentication (run `mwinit` if you encounter authentication errors)

Common use cases:
- Answering technical questions about Amazon internal tools and services
- Providing code examples or troubleshooting steps
- Sharing knowledge about internal processes

Example usage:
{ "questionId": 1234567, "contents": "To solve this issue, you need to run:\n\n```bash\nbrazil workspace merge\n```\n\nThis will resolve the dependency conflicts." }

questionId.description: ID of the question to answer

contents.description: Content of the answer in Markdown format

ToolSpecification.name: overleaf_read_file
ToolSpecification.description:
Read a file from an Overleaf project with automatic synchronization.

This tool reads the specified file from an Overleaf project. Before reading,
it ensures the project is cloned locally and synchronized with the remote repository.
Supports both text and binary files with proper encoding detection.

Example usage:
```json
{
  "project_id": "507f1f77bcf86cd799439011",
  "file_path": "main.tex"
}
```

file_path.description: Path to the file within the project
